preview.canonicalTitle: cross-viewport UI reliability and layout hardening
preview.canonicalBody:
<!-- ATLAS-CARD:START -->
ATLAS-CARD-ID: `mazer-cross-viewport-ui-reliability`
- project: `Mazer`
- type: `feature`
- state: `review`
- priority: `High`
- owner: `Mazer`
- progress: `95%`
- autonomous implementation: `not_admitted`
- updated: `2026-07-15T23:28:12.000Z`

## Summary
Coordinate mobile safe-area, shared overlay geometry, and browser layout through one no-overlap contract and proof matrix.

## Objective
- Make menu, Options, play, and Pause remain readable and operable across phone, browser, PWA, and restored-window surfaces without per-screenshot offsets.

## Acceptance criteria
- Every primary Mazer surface has an owner, supported viewport category, and no-overlap contract.
- Mobile and browser layout work can be verified independently and together through a shared matrix.
- 2 more item(s) in journal/source

## Discoveries
- The latest production screenshots proved the text-bleed fix was real but left labels too small, Pause half-height, e... [see journal/source]
- Smart Steering must retain the held cardinal axis through normal off-axis stick noise before the one-tile lane-shift resolver can operate.

## Next actions
- Obtain explicit production authorization for exact head 8ca79d9a.
- Collect physical-iPhone menu, Options, play, Pause, and Smart Steering proof after release.
- Keep installed-PWA, native browser-chrome, and maximize or restore endpoints open.

## Blockers
- Production still serves the prior explicitly approved 4984d16c artifact.
- Physical-iPhone acceptance for exact head 8ca79d9a has not yet been captured.

## Evidence
- Mazer draft PR #76 at exact head 8ca79d9a843213cecab4eb8bb6963ed2078db603.
- 3 more item(s) in journal/source
<!-- ATLAS-CARD:END -->
preview.journalBody:
ATLAS-JOURNAL-EVENT-ID: `mazer-cross-viewport-ui-reliability-pr76-mobile-fit-steering-review-20260715`
- card: `mazer-cross-viewport-ui-reliability`
- idempotency: `mazer-cross-viewport-ui-reliability-pr76-mobile-fit-steering-review-20260715`
## PR 76 mobile fit and held-axis Smart Steering are proof-ready
- kind: `review`
- state: `review`
- actor: `Mazer`
- occurred: `2026-07-15T23:28:12.000Z`

## Completed
- Enlarged status, action, guide, toggle, and slider text with measured padding and visual minimum-fill gates.
- Matched active-play Pause to the 62px shared status-panel height and moved the scroll cutoff directly below the title.
- Made guide clipping progressive by geometry while removing only complete text rows.
- Locked overlay scrolling during Move Speed drags.
- Preserved held cardinal stick intent through analog noise so one-tile jogs resolve on all four travel directions.

## Discovered
- Physical production evidence remains the final authority for this phone-facing packet.

## Next
- Promote only after explicit operator approval, then collect physical-iPhone screenshots and feel notes.

## Blockers
- Exact head 8ca79d9a is preview-only pending explicit production approval.

## Evidence
- https://github.com/fawxzzy/mazer/pull/76
- Mazer head 8ca79d9a
- Vercel preview dpl_HAcz3TXAnQUca929gqnzHQ87rH7Y
- Capture report C:/ATLAS/tmp/captures/mazer-ui-surfaces/2026-07-15T23-14-52-388Z/report.md

## Correlation
- task: `019f61a0-6b80-7913-b479-f75f2a8a5b77`
- branch: `codex/physical-ui-controls-rework-20260715`
- commit: `8ca79d9a`
- receipt: `mazer-pr76-mobile-fit-steering-review-20260715`

preview.canonicalTitle: normalize browser UI layout across viewports
preview.canonicalBody:
<!-- ATLAS-CARD:START -->
ATLAS-CARD-ID: `mazer-browser-layout-persistence`
- project: `Mazer`
- type: `feature`
- state: `review`
- priority: `High`
- owner: `Mazer`
- progress: `94%`
- autonomous implementation: `not_admitted`
- updated: `2026-07-15T23:28:13.000Z`

## Summary
Normalize responsive browser geometry and preserve only semantic settings so viewport changes cannot replay stale layout.

## Objective
- Recompute all menu, Options, play, and Pause geometry from the active content rectangle while preserving explicit player preferences only.

## Acceptance criteria
- Layout-derived geometry is never persisted as player preference state.
- A recalculated layout cannot overlap, escape the viewport, or hide essential controls.
- Normal reload preserves supported semantic settings with safe migration defaults.
- Narrow, wide, phone, and restore endpoints have deterministic evidence.

## Discoveries
- Guide content needs viewport clipping at the rendering layer, while text rows need clean all-or-nothing visibility to avoid chopped copy.
- The Move Speed slider must own its active pointer so responsive overlay scrolling cannot consume the same gesture.

## Next actions
- Review exact head 8ca79d9a through physical mobile Safari after explicit production promotion.
- Complete native browser-chrome and maximize or restore evidence.

## Blockers
- Anonymous remote screenshot automation is redirected by Vercel team SSO; protection was preserved rather than weakened.
- Physical browser-chrome review remains pending.

## Evidence
- Mazer draft PR #76 at exact head 8ca79d9a.
- Deterministic 390x844 at 3x capture passed Options/Pause top and bottom scroll endpoints, containment, fade-clearance, reachability, and console gates.
- Canonical preview dpl_HAcz3TXAnQUca929gqnzHQ87rH7Y is Ready and serves the same hashed app artifact as the locally captured build.
<!-- ATLAS-CARD:END -->

preview.canonicalTitle: shared menu and play run status panel
preview.canonicalBody:
<!-- ATLAS-CARD:START -->
ATLAS-CARD-ID: `mazer-shared-run-status-panel`
- project: `Mazer`
- type: `feature`
- state: `review`
- priority: `High`
- owner: `Mazer`
- progress: `36%`
- autonomous implementation: `not_admitted`
- updated: `2026-07-15T23:28:14.000Z`

## Summary
Use one responsive menu and play status component for time, Rank or AI Rank, Score out of 100, and Maze level from the correct context.

## Objective
- Give menu and active play the same compact status language while keeping score authority traceable to the canonical scorer.

## Acceptance criteria
- Menu and play consume the same status-panel formatting and layout contract.
- Panel text remains centered, contained, and readable at phone and desktop widths.
- Panel width, gap, and play Pause placement satisfy shared viewport diagnostics.
- Displayed score is traceable to canonical run-quality data rather than a presentation-only calculation.

## Discoveries
- The physically contained status copy was still undersized relative to its available chrome.
- Active-play Pause needs to consume the exact shared status-panel height rather than its former control-size-derived height.

## Next actions
- Review the enlarged shared status text and equal-height Pause control on a physical iPhone after explicit production promotion.
- Consume the finalized canonical 0-to-100 scorer field before completion.

## Blockers
- Physical-phone presentation proof for exact head 8ca79d9a is pending.
- Canonical score authority remains a separate non-terminal dependency.

## Evidence
- Shared compact status font increased to 14px and the wide font to 15px with measured fit padding.
- The deterministic phone capture and visual diagnostic prove exact 62px status/Pause height parity.
- Focused verification passed 85 of 85 and full verification passed 364 of 364.
<!-- ATLAS-CARD:END -->
preview.journalBody:
